name: Nile Red
plain_summary: Solvatochromic phenoxazone dye used to stain lipids; colour and λ_max shift strongly with solvent polarity — classic teaching example.

abs.plain_caption: Strong visible band near ~550 nm in methanol (solvatochromic; shifts with solvent).
abs.lit: Teaching envelope pinned to tabulated Nile Red λ_max in polar solvents (~520–560 nm depending on solvent). Verify primary dye tables before quantitative use.
abs.quality_note: Tier A teaching spectrum — solvatochromism demo
